hub_subtitle: Business English para Gestão de Riscos e Finanças

lesson.menu_title: The Audit Report — Reading a Formal Risk Document
lesson.menu_desc: Reading an internal audit report before it is submitted to the regulator, in the passive voice -- 32 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 6 -- The Audit Report: Reading a Formal Risk Document
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Sergio Takashi Tanaka | Lesson 6 -- The Audit Report

listenings.text: Hello, Sergio. This is Beatriz from internal audit, with a short summary before you read the full report. The desk's controls were reviewed last week, and two deficiencies were found. A limit was breached in March, and the breach was not disclosed to the committee on the same day. The monthly statement was also submitted late twice. A remediation plan is now required, and it must be approved by the committee before the next quarter. The report will be submitted to the regulator, so please make sure your comments are added by Friday. Thank you.

Hi Sergio, it is Paulo from the credit desk. A quick note on the audit. I have read the draft, and the findings look fair to me. The late statement was already fixed, and the new control was tested yesterday. For the limit breach, a remediation plan is being written now, and it will be reviewed on Monday. One thing: the minutes of the March meeting were not attached, so they must be added before the report is submitted. If the plan is approved, the case will be closed without a penalty. Can you check the minutes before lunch? Thanks.

extra_audio.text: First, the desk's controls were examined by the internal audit team. Two deficiencies were identified: a late statement and an undisclosed limit breach. A remediation plan was then prepared by the desk and approved by the committee. Next, the report was submitted to the regulator, and the minutes of the meeting were enclosed. Finally, the actions were completed on time, so no penalty was applied.

reading.rtitle: Internal Audit Report — Risk Desk Controls
paras: This report was prepared by the internal audit team and will be submitted to the regulator next week. During the review, the desk's controls were examined and three areas were tested. In the base case, the controls are considered adequate, but two deficiencies were identified.

First, a limit was breached in March, and the breach was not disclosed to the committee on the same day. Second, the monthly statement was submitted late twice. These deficiencies are rated as medium risk, and remediation is required before the next quarter.

A remediation plan must be prepared by the desk and approved by the committee. The plan will be reviewed by the regulator, and the minutes of the meeting will be enclosed. If the actions are completed on time, no penalty will be applied. The main message is clear: when a limit is breached, it must be disclosed at once.
reading.source: Internal Audit -- Risk Desk Report (adapted for class)

gist.prompt: Read once for the main idea. What is the best title for this report?
choices: A perfect year on the desk, with no problems found
An audit that found two control problems and requires a fix
A regulator's decision to close the risk desk

items: The report was prepared by the internal audit team.
It says the report was prepared by the internal audit team and will be submitted to the regulator.
No deficiencies were found during the review.
Two deficiencies were identified: a late statement and an undisclosed limit breach.
The limit breach was disclosed to the committee on the same day.
The breach was not disclosed to the committee on the same day.
A remediation plan must be approved by the committee.
It says the plan must be prepared by the desk and approved by the committee.
